summary: The result may contain every combination of one row from each school, so its worst-case abc output size matches the cross-join filter's abc candidate checks and leaves no genuine slower principal class to calibrate.

optimality.argument: When all IDs and names are distinct across the three schools, every Cartesian triplet is valid and must be emitted, requiring Omega(abc) output work; the query examines each candidate once with six constant-time comparisons.

replacement_checks.evidence: Tables with globally distinct IDs and names produce exactly abc valid output rows, so any correct query must perform at least proportional output work.
The three-way Cartesian product has abc rows and the query applies three ID and three name inequalities to each row before projection.
Regression compares SQLite query output with an independent Python Cartesian oracle across full-product, empty-result, pairwise ID collision, pairwise name collision, and partial-filter fixtures.
